title: Grease on Denim
timeEstimate: 20–45 minutes; powder preload + Dawn, ring-risk on dark denim
stainChemistry: Grease is heavy oil — cooking grease, butter, animal fat, or mechanic / petroleum grease. The oil sits on the fiber and resists water because oil and water don't mix. Hot water plus heat at any step turns soft oil into a harder, polymerized residue that locks into denim permanently. Denim adds two complications: indigo dye sits on the surface of the fiber, so any aggressive oxidizer (chlorine bleach, concentrated peroxide) strips color along with the stain, and aggressive scrubbing on dark denim leaves a halo ring around the spot. Mechanic / petroleum grease is heavier and more tenacious than cooking grease — it usually needs a citrus-solvent step (Goo Gone) home users skip.
whyThisWorks: Cornstarch or baking soda preload pulls bulk oil up before any moisture is added. Dawn (engineered for cooking grease) emulsifies remaining oil. For petroleum / mechanic grease, Goo Gone on a cloth dissolves what Dawn can't reach. Cool wash plus air-dry prevents heat from polymerizing residual oil into the denim. Indigo stays intact because no chlorine bleach is involved. The honest cap on denim: deep-set old grease and hot-dried grease often plateau and leave a permanent shadow.
defaultAssumption: Cooking-grease splash from kitchen / takeout / pizza on jeans, fresh and not yet hot-dried. Mechanic / petroleum grease = same protocol but Goo Gone step is mandatory. Raw / selvage denim = pro from minute one (any moisture causes uneven fading). Hot-dried grease = often permanent shadow; manage expectations.

spottingProtocol.technique: Pull bulk oil out before water
spottingProtocol.instruction: Cover the grease spot with cornstarch or baking soda. Press gently into the spot. 20–30 min. Soft-brush off. Most surface oil comes off this way without water — water before powder spreads the stain into a wider ring on denim.
Generous Dawn directly on the spot. Soft toothbrush 10 min. Always blot, never rub — denim halos under aggressive scrubbing. Cool damp-cloth rinse.

spottingProtocol.agent: Goo Gone Cloth Tamp for Petroleum Grease
spottingProtocol.technique: Citrus solvent for heavy / mechanic grease
spottingProtocol.equipment: Goo Gone on white cloth, ventilation
spottingProtocol.instruction: Petroleum / mechanic grease only. Apply Goo Gone to a clean white cloth — never pour on denim. Tamp the spot from the top. Hidden-seam test on dark / raw denim first. Open windows; flammable, no flame.

spottingProtocol.equipment: Washing machine, cool water, drying rack
spottingProtocol.instruction: Cool machine wash with normal detergent. AIR-DRY. Inspect dry — wet denim hides residual oil that reappears once dry. NEVER hot dryer until fully clear — heat polymerizes residual oil into denim permanently.

steps: 1. Cornstarch / baking soda preload 20–30 min. Soft-brush off.
2. Generous Dawn pretreat, cool, 10 min, soft toothbrush.
3. Goo Gone cloth tamp for petroleum / mechanic grease, hidden-seam test on dark denim.
4. Cool machine wash + AIR-DRY. Inspect dry before any heat.
products: Goo Gone / citrus solvent (petroleum grease only)
professionalProtocol.temperature: cool throughout — heat polymerizes oil into denim permanently
warnings: NEVER hot water — polymerizes oil into denim
NEVER hot dryer until stain is fully clear
NEVER chlorine bleach on denim — strips indigo unevenly
Always blot, never rub — denim rings under scrubbing
Hidden-seam test before Goo Gone on dark / raw denim
Powder preload BEFORE water — water-first spreads oil into a ring

homeSolutions.agent: Powder First, Water Second — Don't Skip the Order
homeSolutions.instruction: Grease on denim is a tractable home stain IF you do the powder step first. Cornstarch or baking soda pulls bulk oil up before any water touches it. If you wet the denim first, the oil spreads into a wider ring that's much harder to clean. Two hard rules: NO hot water (polymerizes oil into denim permanently) and NO chlorine bleach (strips indigo unevenly, leaves a halo).

homeSolutions.agent: Cornstarch or Baking Soda — 20 to 30 Minutes
homeSolutions.instruction: Cover the grease spot with cornstarch, baking soda, or plain baby powder. Press gently into the spot. 20 to 30 minutes. Soft-brush off. Most surface oil comes off this way without water. Repeat with fresh powder if the first round saturates.

Apply a generous amount of Dawn dish soap directly on the spot. Work in with a soft toothbrush for 10 minutes — Dawn is engineered for cooking grease. Always blot, never rub — rubbing causes a halo on dark denim. Cool damp-cloth rinse.

homeSolutions.agent: Goo Gone for Petroleum or Mechanic Grease
homeSolutions.instruction: If the grease is from a car, bike chain, or mechanical work — heavier than cooking grease — Dawn alone will plateau. Apply Goo Gone (citrus solvent) to a clean white cloth, tamp the spot from the top. Never pour on the denim. Hidden-seam test on dark or raw denim first — Goo Gone can stress dye on some indigo. Open windows; flammable, no flame nearby.

Cool machine wash with normal detergent. AIR-DRY only. Inspect dry — wet denim hides residual oil that reappears once it's fully dry. NEVER hot dryer until the spot is fully clear. Heat at any step polymerizes the remaining oil into the denim permanently and leaves a darker shadow.

steps: 1. NO hot water. NO chlorine bleach.
2. Cornstarch / baking soda preload 20–30 min. Brush off softly.
3. Generous Dawn pretreat, cool, 10 min, soft toothbrush. Blot, don't rub.
4. Goo Gone cloth tamp for petroleum / mechanic grease, hidden-seam test on dark denim.
5. Cool machine wash + AIR-DRY. Inspect dry before any heat.
diyProtocol.whenToStop: Stop if hot water or hot dryer was already used (often permanent shadow). Stop on raw / selvage denim from minute one. Stop on dark denim if hidden test shows indigo loss before Goo Gone. Stop after 2 home rounds without progress.
warnings: NEVER hot dryer until stain clear
NEVER chlorine bleach — strips indigo unevenly
Powder BEFORE water — water-first spreads oil into a ring
Always blot, never rub — denim halos
Hidden-seam test before Goo Gone on dark / raw denim

neverDo: Never hot water on grease + denim
Never hot dryer on residual grease
Never rub denim during cleaning — causes ring
Never water-first on grease — spreads stain
fiberSensitivities: Indigo / dark denim: hidden-seam test before Goo Gone
Raw / selvage denim: pro from minute one — moisture causes uneven fading
Light-wash denim: most forgiving — full home protocol applicable
Stretch denim with elastane: cool only — Goo Gone can stress elastane
Black overdyed denim: pro — color-loss risk on solvent + scrubbing

materialWarnings: Powder preload is the unlock — never water-first
Heat polymerizes oil into denim permanently
No chlorine bleach on denim — strips indigo
Always blot, never rub — denim halos under scrubbing
Raw / selvage denim = pro from minute one

escalation.when: Hot water or hot dryer already used (often permanent shadow). Raw / selvage / black-overdyed denim. Hidden-seam test shows indigo loss before Goo Gone. Petroleum / mechanic grease that didn't respond to Goo Gone after 2 passes. Halo ring already forming. Old set grease through multiple wash cycles.
escalation.whatToTell: "Grease on [light-wash / dark / indigo / raw / selvage] denim, type [cooking / butter / petroleum / mechanic], age [fresh / days / hot-dried suspected], home-treated with [cornstarch preload + Dawn cool + Goo Gone + cool wash + air-dry]. No hot, no chlorine. [Residual shadow / halo ring / indigo loss / hot-dried suspected]."
escalation.specialistType: professional dry cleaner with denim experience plus dry-side solvent capability for petroleum grease

contextQuestions: Cooking grease, butter, animal fat, or petroleum / mechanic grease?
Light-wash, dark indigo, raw / selvage, or black-overdyed denim?
Fresh, days old, or hot-dried already?
Has chlorine bleach been used yet?
deepSolveHooks.modifierNotes: Powder preload is the unlock — never water-first on oil + denim. Petroleum / mechanic grease needs the Goo Gone step that home users often skip. Indigo dye loss is the recurring trap; hidden-seam test before any solvent. Raw / selvage denim is pro from minute one because any moisture causes uneven fading. Hot-dried grease leaves a permanent shadow most of the time; manage expectations.
